howItWorks.intro: This tariff savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Current Total = Annual Spend × (1 + Current Tariff%)
Alt Total = (Annual Spend × (1 + Price Premium%)) × (1 + Alt Tariff%)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How can I reduce tariff costs?
faq.answer: Options include: sourcing from free trade agreement (FTA) countries, using foreign trade zones, tariff engineering (reclassifying products), first-sale valuation, and applying for tariff exclusions.

faq.question: What are the major US free trade agreements?
faq.answer: USMCA (Canada/Mexico), KORUS (South Korea), CAFTA-DR (Central America/DR), Australia FTA, Singapore FTA, Chile FTA, and others. Products qualifying under these may enter duty-free.

faq.question: Is it worth switching suppliers to save on tariffs?
faq.answer: If savings exceed 5-10% of annual spend, it's usually worth investigating. Factor in quality differences, lead times, minimum orders, intellectual property risks, and supplier transition costs.
